dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6297 IH: Protecting Europe from Antisemitic Crime and Extremism Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Fine (for himself and Mr. Miller of Ohio) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Department of State to provide briefings on antisemitism in Europe.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Europe from Antisemitic Crime and Extremism Act or the PEACE Act.

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: the Assistant Secretary for European and Eurasian Affairs, in consultation with other relevant officials of the Department of State, should assess the persistent and growing threat of antisemitism and acts of international terrorism in Europe as a matter of importance to the foreign policy of the United States; and

the Under Secretary for Political Affairs, acting through the Assistant Secretary for European and Eurasian Affairs, should diplomatically engage governments of countries of apparent concern on efforts for transatlantic cooperation to counter and address antisemitism and acts of international terrorism that may threaten transatlantic stability, the safety and security of United States citizens, and institutions abroad.

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, and annually thereafter for two years, the Assistant Secretary for European and Eurasian Affairs shall provide a briefing to the appropriate congressional committees on the matters described in subsection (a).
In this section, the term appropriate congressional committees means—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives; and
the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.
